compiled_state.title: As a developer, centrality computation is cached and large changesets bypass it
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add caching for centrality results keyed by file path and diff hash to avoid redundant ast-grep invocations across repeated record-test-status.sh calls within the same changeset. Add a file count threshold that skips centrality computation entirely when too many files are staged.

**Why**: ast-grep invocation per staged file adds 10-20s wall-clock time for 20 files. Caching avoids redundant computation on re-invocations (e.g., after SIGURG resume). The file count threshold provides a fast-path for large changesets where running the full suite is the obvious choice regardless of centrality.

**Scope**:
- IN: Per-file per-diff-hash caching of centrality scores, test_gate.file_count_threshold config key (default: 50), cache file cleanup when diff hash changes
- OUT: Cross-session cache persistence, cache size limits beyond diff-hash keying

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, centrality results are cached per-file per diff hash to avoid redundant ast-grep computation
  ← Satisfies: "Results are cached per-file per diff hash"
- When this story is complete, changesets exceeding test_gate.file_count_threshold (default: 50) staged files skip centrality computation entirely and run the full suite directly
  ← Satisfies: "When staged file count exceeds threshold, skip centrality and run full suite"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] Cache invalidation per diff hash — files must not grow unbounded; old caches should be cleaned when diff hash changes
- [Performance] First-run cost is 10-20s for 20 files; caching mitigates on subsequent runs within the same changeset
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
